edge_case_description: Standard patient — all common fields populated
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names, DOB, SSN, Address, Phone, Email, MRN, Account#
pii_detection_challenge: Baseline — all fields in standard format
ins1_payer_name: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Texas
detection_notes: Baseline row — all PII in expected canonical formats

edge_case_description: Maiden name vs married name — same SSN different last name
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names (2 variants), SSN, DOB
pii_detection_challenge: Same patient appears as 'Sarah Nguyen' in prior records and 'Sarah Johnson' in current — different strings, same SSN links them
detection_notes: str.replace('Sarah Johnson') will NOT catch 'Sarah Nguyen' — requires cross-reference by SSN or MRN. Both name forms are PII.
redaction_challenge_notes: Must replace BOTH maiden and married name forms consistently
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: Prior-name cross-reference records are themselves PHI

edge_case_description: Hyphenated surname
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names, DOB, Phone
pii_detection_challenge: Hyphen in surname changes string — 'Smith-Jones' ≠ 'Smith Jones' ≠ 'SmithJones' — three distinct strings for same person
detection_notes: Hyphenated names require regex-aware detection: Smith-Jones | Smith Jones | SmithJones all possible in legacy systems

edge_case_description: Apostrophe in surname (O'Brien)
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names, DOB
pii_detection_challenge: Apostrophe causes encoding issues — O'Brien vs OBrien vs O Brien in different systems
detection_notes: Apostrophe often stripped in legacy systems — O'Brien → OBrien → 'OBrien'. Requires apostrophe-tolerant matching

edge_case_description: Hispanic dual surname (paternal + maternal)
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names, DOB, Address
pii_detection_challenge: Dual surname 'Garcia Lopez' may be split, reversed, or combined as 'Garcialopez' in different systems
detection_notes: Dual surname detection requires first-name/surname boundary disambiguation. 'Garcia Lopez' ≠ 'Lopez Garcia' in some records

edge_case_description: Name with generational suffix (Jr, III)
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names, DOB, SSN
pii_detection_challenge: Suffix creates name variants — 'Johnson Jr' vs 'Johnson' vs 'Johnson, Robert Jr' across systems
detection_notes: Suffix (Jr/Sr/III) often stored in separate field — when concatenated, creates variant: 'Johnson III' vs 'Johnson Jr.'

edge_case_description: Preferred / chosen name differs from legal name
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names (2 sets), DOB, Phone
pii_detection_challenge: Legal: 'William Thompson' but goes by 'Billy Thompson' — clinical notes use preferred name, billing uses legal
detection_notes: Clinical notes use 'Billy' — detection must cover both preferred and legal names as separate PII instances
redaction_challenge_notes: Replace 'Billy Thompson' AND 'William Thompson' consistently with same synthetic person

edge_case_description: Transgender patient — legal name differs from historical records
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names (prior+current), DOB, Gender identity fields
pii_detection_challenge: Prior records show 'Michael Torres' (deadname); current records show 'Michelle Torres' — same SSN + DOB links them
detection_notes: Prior legal name (deadname) is PHI under HIPAA — must be redacted from historical records. gender_identity and sex_assigned_at_birth are sensitive PHI.
redaction_challenge_notes: Deadname and current name must both be redacted consistently. Gender identity fields are HIPAA-protected PHI.
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: Transgender status itself is highly sensitive PHI — combination of deadname + new name is re-identification vector

edge_case_description: Minor patient — parent is subscriber and guarantor
hipaa_identifiers_present: Patient name, DOB; Parent name, SSN, DOB; Insurance under parent
pii_detection_challenge: Three different people: minor patient, parent/subscriber, parent/guarantor (same parent here — but 2 people's PII on one claim)
detection_notes: Minor typically has no SSN on file — billing uses parent subscriber ID. Parent SSN appears as both subscriber SSN AND guarantor SSN on same record.
redaction_challenge_notes: Parent's SSN appears in both ins1_subscriber_ssn AND guarantor_ssn — must replace consistently with same synthetic value

edge_case_description: Newborn billed under mother's insurance ID with suffix
hipaa_identifiers_present: Newborn name+DOB; Mother's member ID with suffix code
pii_detection_challenge: Newborn uses mother's member ID + suffix '01' before receiving own ID — detecting suffix-modified member IDs
detection_notes: Member ID 'YMT987654321-01' = mother's ID + newborn suffix. Pattern-matching on raw member ID may miss suffix variant.
redaction_challenge_notes: Suffix '-01' must be replaced consistently — synthetic mother ID + same suffix

edge_case_description: Deceased patient — date of death present; estate representative
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names, DOB, Date of Death, Estate rep PII
pii_detection_challenge: PHI of deceased persons remains protected 50 years post-death; estate rep adds 3rd-party PII
detection_notes: patient_dod is HIPAA identifier (C — dates). Age >90 must be aggregated per Safe Harbor. Estate rep name+SSN+DOB = additional PHI on claim.
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: DOB must be generalized to year only; DOD must be removed; age reported as '>90'

edge_case_description: Age 90+ — HIPAA Safe Harbor requires aggregation
hipaa_identifiers_present: DOB (requires suppression), Age (requires '>90' generalization)
pii_detection_challenge: HIPAA Safe Harbor requires all ages >89 be aggregated as '90+' — detection system must flag, not just redact
detection_notes: DOB 1928 exposes age >90 — must suppress full DOB, replace age with '>90'. Year-of-birth alone in combination with other demographics is re-identification risk.
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: DOB → year only (1928); Age → '>90'; all geographic data smaller than state

edge_case_description: BCBS member ID — standard format with state prefix
hipaa_identifiers_present: Member ID (Health plan beneficiary #), Group #, Subscriber PII
pii_detection_challenge: BCBS IDs have 3-char alphanumeric prefix + numeric suffix — must distinguish from other ID formats
ins1_payer_name: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Texas
detection_notes: BCBS member ID format: [A-Z0-9]{3}[0-9]{6,12}. FEP (Federal Employee) variant: R + 8 digits. Rx BIN/PCN/Group are separate sensitive IDs on pharmacy card.

edge_case_description: Aetna member ID — W-prefix numeric format
hipaa_identifiers_present: Member ID, Group #
pii_detection_challenge: Aetna IDs: 'W' prefix + 8-12 digits. May also appear as purely numeric without W prefix in different systems.
detection_notes: Aetna ID: W + 8-12 digits OR purely numeric 8-12 digits. 'W123456789' is a member ID — context-aware detection required to distinguish from random numbers.

edge_case_description: UnitedHealthcare — 9-11 digit numeric member ID
hipaa_identifiers_present: Member ID, Group #
pii_detection_challenge: UHC IDs are purely numeric — easily confused with phone numbers, account numbers, dates
detection_notes: UHC 9-digit member ID '987654321' is visually identical to SSN without dashes. Context/field-label detection critical to avoid false negatives.

edge_case_description: Medicare MBI — 11-character alphanumeric
hipaa_identifiers_present: MBI (Health plan beneficiary #), Name, DOB
pii_detection_challenge: MBI format: C-AN-N-AN-N-AN-AN-N-N (no S,L,O,I,B,Z). Visually distinct from commercial IDs but pattern-matching required.
detection_notes: MBI regex: ^[1-9][AC-HJKMNP-RT-Y][A-Z0-9]\d[AC-HJKMNP-RT-Y][A-Z0-9]\d[AC-HJKMNP-RT-Y]{2}\d{2}$. Letters B,D,I,L,O,S,Z excluded.
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: MBI is health plan beneficiary number (identifier H) — must be fully removed

edge_case_description: Dual-eligible — Medicare MBI + Medicaid CIN on same claim
hipaa_identifiers_present: MBI + Medicaid ID, Name, DOB (dual IDs)
pii_detection_challenge: Dual-eligible claims carry two insurance IDs — both are PHI. Medicaid CIN format varies by state.
detection_notes: Dual-eligible: Medicare MBI (format: 2CD5EF6GH78) AND Medicaid CIN (TX-MAID-1234567890) must both be detected and replaced with consistent synthetic IDs.

edge_case_description: TRICARE — military beneficiary
hipaa_identifiers_present: DoD ID (11-digit), Name, DOB, Sponsor SSN
pii_detection_challenge: TRICARE uses DoD Beneficiary ID or SSN-based ID. Sponsor (active duty member) SSN appears as policy basis.
detection_notes: TRICARE DoD Beneficiary ID is 11-digit number. Military address (APO/FPO) is geographic PII. Sponsor SSN appears as insurance basis.

edge_case_description: Self-pay — no insurance; guarantor is patient
hipaa_identifiers_present: Patient PII only, no insurance fields
pii_detection_challenge: Self-pay records have no member ID but still contain full patient demographics. Guarantor = patient (self).
detection_notes: Self-pay: no member ID to detect, but all patient demographics still PHI. Guarantor_ssn = patient_ssn — deduplication requires canonical-value awareness.

edge_case_description: Primary + secondary insurance (2-way COB)
hipaa_identifiers_present: 2× subscriber PII, 2× member IDs, 2× group numbers
pii_detection_challenge: Two insurers = two complete sets of subscriber PII. Primary subscriber may differ from secondary.
detection_notes: COB: 2 subscriber names (different people), 2 SSNs, 2 member IDs, 2 DOBs, 2 group numbers. Secondary subscriber (spouse) is 3rd-party PII.
redaction_challenge_notes: ins1_subscriber_ssn and ins2_subscriber_ssn must map to different synthetic SSNs since they represent different people

edge_case_description: Three-way COB — primary + secondary + tertiary
hipaa_identifiers_present: 3× subscriber PII, 3× member IDs
pii_detection_challenge: Three insurers triples insurance PII; UB-04 FL 50-65 has A/B/C lanes for all three payers
detection_notes: Three-way COB: UB-04 supports 3 payer lines (FL 50-65 A/B/C). Three member IDs, three group numbers, three authorization codes — all PHI.
redaction_challenge_notes: ins3_member_id is tertiary and may be missed if detection only handles primary/secondary fields

edge_case_description: Medicare primary + Medicaid secondary (dual-eligible claim)
hipaa_identifiers_present: MBI + Medicaid ID, dual subscriber PII
pii_detection_challenge: Medicare-crossover claim: paid by Medicare first, then crosses to Medicaid automatically. Two ID formats on same claim.
detection_notes: Crossover claim: MBI format (4GH7IJ8KL90) AND Medicaid CIN (LA-9087654321) — two structurally different insurance IDs on one claim.

edge_case_description: All three are different people — grandmother, parent employer plan, minor patient
hipaa_identifiers_present: 3× Names, 3× DOBs, 2× SSNs, address for each
pii_detection_challenge: Three distinct individuals on one claim — naive detection may only extract one person's data
detection_notes: Three people: patient (Tyler, age 8, no SSN), subscriber (Kevin — father, has SSN), guarantor (Linda — grandmother, has own SSN). Emergency contact = subscriber (father) — name duplication.
redaction_challenge_notes: guarantor_ssn ≠ subscriber_ssn — must map to two different synthetic SSNs. guarantor_name ≠ subscriber_name — two different people

edge_case_description: Deceased estate — patient deceased, estate rep as guarantor
hipaa_identifiers_present: Deceased patient PII, estate rep name+SSN+address
pii_detection_challenge: Billing goes to estate representative — adds another person's complete demographics to record
detection_notes: Deceased patient: DOD is HIPAA C identifier. Estate rep is 3rd-party person adding name+DOB+SSN+address+phone+email to the record.
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: DOB (generalize to year 1928), DOD (remove), age '>90'; estate rep address city/state/zip also covered under (B)

edge_case_description: Solo practice — billing = rendering = ordering, same NPI
hipaa_identifiers_present: Single provider NPI, DEA, state license, EIN/SSN as Tax ID
pii_detection_challenge: Solo provider: one NPI serves billing and rendering roles. Tax ID may be SSN (sole proprietor).
detection_notes: Sole proprietor uses SSN as Federal Tax ID on CMS-1500 Box 25. billing_provider_tax_id_ssn is SSN — must be redacted. DEA number BW1234563 validates (B=practitioner, W=Whitfield).
redaction_challenge_notes: SSN as Tax ID is easy to miss — appears in provider section, not patient section

edge_case_description: Group practice — billing NPI (Type 2) ≠ rendering NPI (Type 1)
hipaa_identifiers_present: Two different NPIs, EIN for org, SSN/license for individual
pii_detection_challenge: Billing entity (group) has Type 2 NPI + EIN; Rendering provider has Type 1 NPI + DEA + state license — distinct PII sets
detection_notes: Org NPI (Type 2) + EIN for billing; Individual NPI (Type 1) + DEA + license for rendering. Referring provider adds 3rd NPI. Three provider NPIs, one EIN, one DEA, one license.

edge_case_description: Full provider chain — referring + attending + rendering + operating (surgical case)
hipaa_identifiers_present: 5 provider names, 4-5 NPIs, DEA, state license
pii_detection_challenge: Surgical UB-04: attending ≠ operating ≠ rendering ≠ referring. Each has distinct NPI + PII.
detection_notes: 5 provider names and 5 NPIs on one institutional claim. Attending=Rendering (same NPI appears twice). Anesthesiologist is 'other provider' — may be missed.
redaction_challenge_notes: Same NPI 1098765432 in both attending AND rendering fields — must replace with same synthetic NPI consistently

edge_case_description: Pharmacy / Rx claim (NCPDP format) — full prescriber + pharmacy PII
hipaa_identifiers_present: Patient PII, Prescriber name+NPI+DEA, Pharmacy name+NCPDP+NPI, NDC, Rx#, BIN/PCN
pii_detection_challenge: Pharmacy claims uniquely combine patient demographics with prescriber AND pharmacy PII + drug-level identifiers
detection_notes: NDC 00777-3105-02 links drug to patient — indirectly reveals diagnosis (MS). NCPDP pharmacy ID + NPI + DEA are distinct pharmacy identifiers. Rx# is unique identifier.
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: NDC for specialty drug (Avonex for MS) reveals diagnosis even if ICD-10 absent — quasi-identifier risk

edge_case_description: Workers' compensation claim — employer + carrier + attorney PII
hipaa_identifiers_present: Patient PII, Employer name+address+EIN+contact, WC carrier+policy#, Jurisdiction claim#, Attorney PII
pii_detection_challenge: Workers comp adds 3 new PII entities: employer, WC carrier, claimant attorney. Injury description may reveal occupation.
wc_injury_description: Crushing injury to right hand — operating table saw at job site
detection_notes: Injury description reveals occupation (construction worker, table saw). WC employer EIN is PII. Attorney bar number is unique identifier (HIPAA K). Four distinct entities with PII.
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: Injury description + employer + date of injury combination may uniquely identify incident even without patient name

edge_case_description: Mental health claim — 42 CFR Part 2 substance use extra protection
hipaa_identifiers_present: Patient PII, SUD diagnosis, Provider PII
pii_detection_challenge: Substance use disorder (SUD) records under 42 CFR Part 2 require stricter protection than HIPAA alone. Diagnosis reveals sensitive condition.
detection_notes: F10.20 (alcohol use disorder, uncomplicated) reveals sensitive SUD condition — 42 CFR Part 2 applies, stricter than HIPAA. F33.1 (MDD moderate) is mental health PHI.
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: Behavioral health diagnosis codes are highly sensitive quasi-identifiers — SUD diagnosis + demographic combination is rare enough to re-identify

edge_case_description: Dental claim (ADA form) — dentist NPI + license + dental-specific fields
hipaa_identifiers_present: Patient PII, Dentist NPI + license, Tooth numbers, Dental procedure codes
pii_detection_challenge: ADA dental claims use CDT codes (D-codes), tooth numbers/surfaces — unique to dental domain; tooth chart data can be forensic identifier
detection_notes: ADA dental claim: tooth number (e.g., #14) and tooth surface data are dental records — unique identifiers under HIPAA (R — other unique characteristic). CDT codes are procedure-specific.
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: Dental charts with tooth numbers are forensic identifiers — highly unique to individual

edge_case_description: Date of birth across 8 format variants — same patient, same value
hipaa_identifiers_present: DOB (C — dates)
pii_detection_challenge: Single DOB value appears in 8 different string forms across different fields — each is a distinct string
detection_notes: DOB variants: 04/22/1975 | 1975-04-22 | 04/22/75 | April 22, 1975 | 19750422 | Apr 22, 1975 | 22-Apr-75 | **/**/1975. Each is a distinct string — str.replace on one format misses all others.
redaction_challenge_notes: Must detect all format variants and replace consistently: if DOB maps to June 15, 1980 synthetic, ALL variants must reflect June 15, 1980 in their respective formats

edge_case_description: Service date range, admission, discharge, auth expiry — 5 date types one claim
hipaa_identifiers_present: Multiple date instances (C — dates)
pii_detection_challenge: One institutional claim contains: DOS range, admission date, discharge date, auth expiry, DOB — 5 distinct date fields, potential 15 format variants
detection_notes: Five date values on one claim: DOB (11/03/1960), DOS from (01/15/2024), DOS to (01/18/2024), admission (01/15), discharge (01/18), auth expiry (03/15/2024). Each must be detected and replaced independently.
redaction_challenge_notes: Discharge date (01/18/2024) - Admission date (01/15/2024) = 3-day stay — replacing dates must maintain internally consistent date arithmetic

edge_case_description: Year-only DOB reference — Safe Harbor edge case
hipaa_identifiers_present: DOB — partial (year only) reference
pii_detection_challenge: Clinical notes contain 'born in 1960' or '**/**/1960' — no day/month so date-pattern regex won't match but still PHI
detection_notes: 'born in 1948' — no standard date pattern matches year-only prose reference. '**/**/1948' — partially masked, year only visible. LLM semantic detection required; regex alone fails.
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: Full DOB must be suppressed; 'born in 1948' is PHI even without month/day; age 75 is retainable (≤89)

edge_case_description: SSN in 7 format variants — standard, no-dash, masked, last-4, ITIN
hipaa_identifiers_present: SSN (G — Social Security numbers)
pii_detection_challenge: Seven SSN/ITIN string forms for same or related values — each a distinct string requiring separate detection
detection_notes: SSN variants: 567-89-0125 | 567890125 | ***-**-0125 | last 4: 0125 | space-separated: 567 89 0125. Each is distinct string. Note: 567890125 (9 digits no dash) visually identical to UHC member ID format.
redaction_challenge_notes: ***-**-0125 must update to ***-**-[new last 4 of synthetic SSN], not just blanked

edge_case_description: ITIN instead of SSN — non-citizen patient
hipaa_identifiers_present: ITIN (G — SSN/Tax ID), Name, DOB
pii_detection_challenge: ITIN starts with 9 (area code 900-999) — distinct from valid SSN. Range: 9XX-50 to 65, 70-88, 90-92, 94-99 for middle digits.
detection_notes: ITIN 912-70-3456 format: 9XX (always starts with 9), middle digits 70 (valid ITIN range). Passport number MEX-A1234567 is a HIPAA unique identifier (R). Regex must differentiate ITIN from SSN.
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: ITIN is Social Security number equivalent (G); passport is certificate/license (K)

edge_case_description: Provider SSN as Tax ID (sole proprietor) in billing section
hipaa_identifiers_present: SSN appearing in provider field (not patient field)
pii_detection_challenge: Provider's SSN appears as billing Tax ID — detection must find SSN regardless of which section/field it appears in
billing_provider_address: 701 Solo Practice Ln, San Antonio TX 78201
detection_notes: Provider SSN 678-90-1235 appears in BILLING section as Tax ID — same format as patient SSN but in different context. Entity-aware detection required. DEA BO1234567: B=practitioner, O=Okonkwo.
redaction_challenge_notes: This SSN belongs to the PROVIDER — replacement must use a new synthetic SSN that is not the patient's synthetic SSN

edge_case_description: NPI — Type 1 individual vs Type 2 organizational validation
hipaa_identifiers_present: NPI (R — unique identifiers), EIN, DEA
pii_detection_challenge: Both NPI types are 10-digit numeric with Luhn check digit — but Type 1=individual, Type 2=org. Context distinguishes them.
detection_notes: NPIs: 1234567893 (org, 10-digit Luhn-valid), 1902837465 (individual), 1987654322 (individual). All same format — context (billing vs rendering vs referring) determines role. Regex: ^[12]\d{9}$
redaction_challenge_notes: Three distinct NPIs on one claim — each must map to a different valid synthetic NPI (Luhn-valid)

edge_case_description: DEA number with validation — format and checksum
hipaa_identifiers_present: DEA (K — certificate/license numbers), NPI, state license
pii_detection_challenge: DEA format: 2 letters + 7 digits. First letter = registrant type; second = first letter of surname. Check digit validated.
detection_notes: DEA BM2345674: B=practitioner, M=Martinez surname initial. Checksum: 2+4+7=13; 2×(3+5+6)=28; 13+28=41; check digit=1? Validate: 2+4+7=13; 2×(3+5+6)=28; total=41; last digit=1. Wait: BM2345674 → 2,3,4,5,6,7,4: odd positions(1,3,5,7)=2+4+6+4=16; even(2,4,6)=3+5+7=15; 16+2×15=46; 46 mod10=6≠4. Use fictitious value for illustration. Format regex: ^[ABFGM][A-Za-z9]\d{7}$

edge_case_description: NDC drug code — 3 format variants (4-4-2, 5-3-2, 5-4-1)
hipaa_identifiers_present: NDC (R — unique identifiers), Drug name as quasi-identifier
pii_detection_challenge: NDC 10-digit code has 3 segment configurations; padded 11-digit for CMS. Drug name reveals diagnosis indirectly.
detection_notes: NDC 00777-3105-02 (4-4-2 format) = 00777310502 (11-digit padded) = 7773105-02 (5-3-2). Drug name 'Tecfidera' reveals MS diagnosis — PHI quasi-identifier. NDC regex: \d{4}-\d{4}-\d{2} | \d{5}-\d{3}-\d{2} | \d{5}-\d{4}-\d{1} | \d{11}
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: Specialty drug NDC (Tecfidera for MS) is indirect diagnosis indicator — quasi-identifier even after NDC is removed

edge_case_description: Prior authorization number + expiry — payer-specific formats
hipaa_identifiers_present: Auth number (R — unique identifiers), Auth expiry date (C — dates)
pii_detection_challenge: Auth number format varies by payer: Aetna uses AUTH-YYYY-XXXXXX; BCBS uses AUTH-TX-XXXXXXXXXX etc.
detection_notes: Prior auth number formats by payer: Aetna AUTH-YYYY-NNNNNN; BCBS AUTH-TX-NNNNNNNNNN; UHC AUTH-NNNNNNNNNN; Humana HAUTH-XXXXXXXX. Each is HIPAA (R) unique identifier.
redaction_challenge_notes: Claim number and auth number must be replaced consistently if they cross-reference each other

edge_case_description: PO Box address vs physical address — same patient
hipaa_identifiers_present: 2 addresses (B — geographic subdivisions)
pii_detection_challenge: Patient has PO Box for mail, physical address for home — two different addresses both are PHI, may appear in different fields
detection_notes: Physical: 892 Countryside Drive, Buda TX 78610. Mailing: PO Box 4521, Buda TX 78610-4521. Two distinct strings for same patient. Billing may use PO Box; service records use physical address.

edge_case_description: Military APO/FPO address — active duty patient
hipaa_identifiers_present: Military address (B — geographic; NOT subject to state/city geographic restrictions)
pii_detection_challenge: APO/FPO addresses reveal military affiliation — sensitive category; standard city/state/ZIP rules don't apply to APO codes
detection_notes: APO AE 09349 = Army Post Office, Europe. City='APO', State='AE' — not a standard US city/state. Military rank prefix (SGT) is PII. International phone format 011-49-xxx.
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: Address is PHI (B). 'APO AE 09349' identifies military installation — geographic identifier.

edge_case_description: Service address ≠ billing address — facility vs provider billing
hipaa_identifiers_present: Two facility addresses (B — geographic)
pii_detection_challenge: Service rendered at clinic (service address) but billed from headquarters (billing address) — two different institutional addresses
detection_notes: Billing address (Arlington) ≠ service facility address (Azle) — two distinct geographic PII entries. CMS-1500 Box 32 = service location; Box 33 = billing location. Both are PHI.

edge_case_description: Same patient phone in 8 formats across different source fields
hipaa_identifiers_present: Phone (D — telephone numbers)
pii_detection_challenge: One phone number 'lives' as 8 distinct strings in different system fields — regex on any one form misses others
redaction_challenge_notes: Normalize all variants to canonical (+1NNNNNNNNNN), look up synthetic, re-apply each format template

edge_case_description: Insurer 800-number and provider pager — different phone categories
hipaa_identifiers_present: Toll-free phone (D), Pager (D), Fax (E)
pii_detection_challenge: 800-numbers, pager codes, and fax numbers are all HIPAA telephone/fax identifiers but different formats
detection_notes: Billing phone (512) 555-3456, fax (512) 555-3457 — adjacent numbers look like one number with +1 but are separate identifiers. Provider pager '(512) 300-1099' vs member services '1-800-555-1234' — both HIPAA telephone identifiers.
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: Telephone numbers (D) and fax numbers (E) are separate HIPAA identifier categories

edge_case_description: Rare disease ICD-10 — Fabry disease (E75.21) as quasi-identifier
hipaa_identifiers_present: Diagnosis (not HIPAA Safe Harbor — but Expert Determination flag)
pii_detection_challenge: Fabry disease affects ~1:40,000 people. E75.21 + minimal demographics uniquely identifies patient despite Safe Harbor compliance.
quasi_id_combo_description: E75.21 (Fabry disease) + male sex + age 45 + Texas = <5 possible individuals statewide
detection_notes: HIPAA Safe Harbor does NOT require removal of diagnosis codes, but E75.21 is a rare disease quasi-identifier. Expert Determination analysis needed. Combination with age/sex/ZIP could identify patient even with name removed.
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: E75.21 alone may constitute re-identification risk — population <1,000 in Texas. Should be generalized to parent code E75.2 or removed under Expert Determination

edge_case_description: HIV status (Z21) — sensitive diagnosis with federal protections
hipaa_identifiers_present: HIV diagnosis, Name, DOB
pii_detection_challenge: HIV status carries additional federal protections beyond HIPAA. Z21 (asymptomatic HIV infection) is highly sensitive.
rendering_provider_name: Dr. Aisha L Osei MD (Infectious Disease)
quasi_id_combo_description: Z21/B20 (HIV) + age + location + prescriber specialty reveals HIV-positive status
quasi_id_reidentification_risk: CRITICAL — Ryan White confidentiality protections apply
detection_notes: Z21 (asymptomatic HIV) + B20 (HIV disease) together confirm HIV status. Provider specialty (Infectious Disease) reinforces diagnosis. Ryan White Program records have additional confidentiality protections beyond HIPAA.
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: HIV diagnosis is sensitive health category — combination with demographics creates extreme re-identification risk

edge_case_description: Huntington's disease (G10) — genetic identifier, family implications
hipaa_identifiers_present: Genetic diagnosis, Name, DOB — family re-identification risk
pii_detection_challenge: Huntington's is hereditary — one diagnosis implies 50% chance of same in first-degree relatives. Family members become quasi-identifiable.
rendering_provider_name: Dr. Sandra M Park MD PhD (Neurology/Genetics)
quasi_id_combo_description: G10 (Huntington's) + surname + family members = re-identifies entire family lineage
quasi_id_reidentification_risk: EXTREME — genetic condition with family re-identification implications
detection_notes: Huntington's disease (G10) affects ~1:10,000. Autosomal dominant — children of patient have 50% risk. Emergency contact surname (Campbell) links family. Emergency contact is 3rd-party PII.
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: Genetic condition diagnoses are ultra-sensitive — Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act (GINA) additional protections apply beyond HIPAA

edge_case_description: Same name + same DOB — two different patients, different SSNs
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names (matching), DOBs (matching), SSNs (different)
pii_detection_challenge: Two patients named 'Michael Johnson' both born 05/20/1968 — different SSNs disambiguate. Redaction must not conflate.
quasi_id_combo_description: First name + Last name + DOB alone do NOT uniquely identify — SSN or MRN required
detection_notes: Two distinct patients: Michael D Johnson SSN 111-22-3344 MRN RGH-2023-011820 vs Michael J Johnson SSN 222-33-4455 MRN RGH-2023-018456. Same name+DOB — different people. Synthetic replacement must produce DIFFERENT new identities for each.
redaction_challenge_notes: If same synthetic 'Michael Johnson' replacement used for both patients, the distinction is lost — must track by MRN or SSN as canonical key

edge_case_description: Same patient — different name forms across same document
hipaa_identifiers_present: Name variants (A — names): full, short, title+last, initials
pii_detection_challenge: Sarah Johnson appears as: 'Sarah Johnson', 'Ms. Johnson', 'Sarah E. Johnson', 'S.E.J.' — 4 different strings, all same person
detection_notes: Four name forms for same patient: 'Sarah Elizabeth Johnson' | 'Sarah Johnson' | 'Ms. Johnson' | 'S.E.J.' | 'Sarah E. Johnson' | 'Sarah' (first name only in prose). Each is a distinct string that str.replace on full name misses.
redaction_challenge_notes: Initials 'S.E.J.' → 'L.C.M.' (if synthetic is Laura Catherine Mitchell). Title+last 'Ms. Johnson' → 'Ms. Mitchell'. Each variant requires its own format-aware replacement

edge_case_description: DICOM study metadata — 10 PII tags in structured fields
hipaa_identifiers_present: All 18 HIPAA identifiers potentially present in DICOM tags
pii_detection_challenge: DICOM header contains 30+ PII tags. Burned-in pixel annotations embed PII as image data — invisible to text scanners.
dicom_burned_in_annotation_flag: YES — T2-FLAIR overlay with patient name and MRN
detection_notes: DICOM Patient Name tag format: LastName^FirstName^Middle^^ — caret-delimited. Patient ID = MRN. Study date in YYYYMMDD (HL7). Burned-in annotations = PII in pixel data, invisible to text-layer scanner; OCR required.
redaction_challenge_notes: DICOM tags require pydicom library to access. Burned-in pixel annotations require OCR + image inpainting for replacement.
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: All 222 DICOM tags addressed by HIPAA Supplement 142. Burned-in annotations covered by identifier Q (full-face photographs and comparable images)

edge_case_description: Telehealth claim — IP address, session data, patient portal URL
hipaa_identifiers_present: IP address (O — IP addresses), URL (N — web URLs), Name, DOB
pii_detection_challenge: Telehealth introduces IP address and URL as PHI under HIPAA. IP reveals location even without address field.
detection_notes: IP address 192.168.10.45 is HIPAA O identifier. Patient portal URL contains MRN 'RGH-2024-039812' — URL itself is HIPAA N identifier. POS 02 = telehealth. Session metadata may include device ID (HIPAA M).
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: IP address (O), URL (N) are both explicit HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers

edge_case_description: International patient — passport as primary ID, no SSN
hipaa_identifiers_present: Passport (K — certificate/license numbers), International address, International phone
pii_detection_challenge: Non-US patient uses passport instead of SSN. Address and phone formats differ. Insurance may be international/travel.
detection_notes: Passport GBR-C89012345 = UK passport number (K identifier). Umlaut in name (Müller) may cause encoding issues. UK phone +44-20-7946-0958. UK postcode W8 5DL = geographic data (B). No SSN — passport is primary government ID.
redaction_challenge_notes: Synthetic replacement must use valid UK passport format (3-letter country + letter + 8 digits). UK postcode requires UK-format synthetic.

edge_case_description: EOB document — reveals service details, provider name, amounts paid
hipaa_identifiers_present: Names, DOB, Member ID, Dates of Service, Provider name+address, Remark codes
pii_detection_challenge: EOB mailed to subscriber reveals patient's care details — sent to household so sensitive for minor/dependent or transgender patient
eob_mailing_address: David Allen Johnson, 4521 Oak Street, Austin TX 78701
eob_service_description: Neurology office visit + MRI brain w/wo contrast
detection_notes: EOB sent to subscriber (David) reveals patient (Sarah) received neurology MRI — implicit MS diagnosis disclosure to household. Subscriber name+address on EOB mailing label is 3rd-party PII.
hipaa_expert_determination_notes: Service description 'MRI brain' on EOB reveals neurological condition — indirectly discloses diagnosis to subscriber/household even if diagnosis code not shown

edge_case_description: Single patient — all PII types in all format variants simultaneously
hipaa_identifiers_present: All 18 HIPAA identifiers in multiple format variants
pii_detection_challenge: Maximum edge case density — same patient's data appearing in every known format variant across all PII categories
ins1_payer_name: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Texas
detection_notes: Maximum density row — same patient in all format variants. 45 PII instances across all 18 HIPAA identifier categories. Use as comprehensive ground-truth benchmark row.
redaction_challenge_notes: All format variants must resolve to same synthetic entity. ins1_subscriber_ssn = guarantor_ssn = patient_ssn — one synthetic SSN serves all three fields.
hipaa_safe_harbor_removes: All 18 identifier categories present: Names(A), Geographic(B), Dates(C), Phone(D), Fax(E), Email(F), SSN(G), MRN(H), Member ID(I), Account#(J), License(K), URL(N), IP(O), Photo/DICOM(Q), Claim#/Auth#(R)
